title: Problem Solving: Organize Data
essential_question: How can you use the strategy make a table to organize data and solve problems?
strategies: Record data in a frequency table
unit_intro_message: U2에 오신 것을 환영합니다! 이 단원에서는 탤리표·빈도표·그림 그래프·막대 그래프로 데이터를 정리하고 읽는 방법을 배웁니다. 데이터에서 패턴과 비교를 찾아내는 능력은 수학적 추론의 핵심입니다.
unit_close_message: U2를 완료했습니다! 탤리표와 빈도표를 읽고 작성하며, 그림 그래프와 막대 그래프를 해석하는 능력을 갖추었습니다. 이제 비교·합산·2단계 문제까지 데이터 분석 전문가가 되었습니다!

pretest.question: A tally table shows: Soccer ||||, Baseball |||, Tennis ||. How many students chose Soccer?
pretest.explanation: Count the tally marks for Soccer: |||| = 4.

expected_errors.description: Soccer 탤리 |||| = 4개를 3으로 잘못 읽음
expected_errors.citation: Friel et al. (2001) p.130 — 탤리 그룹 오독

expected_errors.note: 5로 한 개 더 카운트하는 단순 오류

pretest.math_note: Count the tally marks for Soccer: |||| = 4.

learn.title: What is a Tally Table?
learn.content: A tally table uses tally marks to record data. Each mark | represents 1. A group of five is written as 𝍸 (four marks with a line through them). To find the total, count the tally marks.
learn.math_note: A tally table uses tally marks to record data. Each mark | represents 1. A group

learn.title: What is a Frequency Table?
learn.content: A frequency table uses numbers to show how many times each item was chosen. It has two columns: the category and the number (frequency). You can convert a tally table into a frequency table by counting the tally marks.
learn.math_note: A frequency table uses numbers to show how many times each item was chosen. It h

learn.title: Comparing Data
learn.content: To find 'how many more' or 'how many fewer,' subtract the smaller number from the larger number. To find a total, add all the numbers together.
learn.math_note: To find 'how many more' or 'how many fewer,' subtract the smaller number from th

learn.title: Converting a Tally Table to a Frequency Table
learn.content: To convert a tally table to a frequency table: (1) Look at each row. (2) Count the tally marks — remember: 𝍸 = 5, | = 1. (3) Write the number in the frequency column. Example: 𝍸|| → 5 + 2 = 7.

learn.title: Finding the Total from a Table
learn.content: To find the total number of students surveyed, ADD all the numbers in the frequency column. Write an equation: __ + __ + __ = total. Example: Cats 9, Dogs 7, Fish 4 → 9 + 7 + 4 = 20 students total.
learn.math_note: 합계 = 모든 빈도 더하기. 3개 이상 카테고리도 순서대로 더함.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 2 Lesson 2.1 — Problem Solving: Organize Data

learn.title: Solving Two-Step Data Problems
learn.content: Some problems need TWO steps. Example: 'How many students did NOT choose Pizza?' Step 1: Find the TOTAL (add all categories). Step 2: Subtract the Pizza value from the total. Always re-read the question after Step 1 to plan Step 2.
learn.math_note: 'NOT 선택한 수' = 전체 합 − 해당 카테고리 수. 2단계 계획 필수.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 2 Lesson 2.1 — Problem Solving: Organize Data

learn.title: Math Talk: Check Your Reasoning
learn.content: A frequency table shows: Sunny 9, Rainy 4, Cloudy 7, Snowy 2. Total = 9 + 4 + 7 + 2 = 22 days. How many MORE Sunny days than Snowy days? Check: 9 − 2 = 7. ✓ (Not 9 + 2 = 11!)
learn.math_talk_prompt: 이 표를 보고 계산했습니다: Sunny=9, Snowy=2 → 9−2=7. 친구가 '9+2=11이 맞아요'라고 했습니다. 어떻게 설명하면 친구의 생각을 수정할 수 있을까요? '더 많은'이라는 말은 더하기 기호가 아니라 어떤 연산을 의미하는지 이야기해 봅시다.
learn.math_note: 'How many more' = 큰 수 − 작은 수 = 차이. 9−2=7 ✓

learn.title: Lesson Summary: Organize Data
learn.content: In this lesson you learned how to: ① Read tally marks (𝍸=5, |=1). ② Convert a tally table to a frequency table. ③ Find totals by adding all frequency values. ④ Compare categories using subtraction ('how many more/fewer'). ⑤ Solve two-step problems (find total → subtract one category).
learn.lesson_summary: 탤리표·빈도표 마스터: ①𝍸=5 탤리 읽기 ②탤리→빈도 변환 ③모든 빈도 합산 = 전체 ④'더 많은/적은' = 뺄셈 ⑤2단계 문제: 전체합→특정 카테고리 빼기
learn.math_note: 핵심: 비교 문제는 항상 빼기. 2단계 문제는 전체 구한 후 빼기.

practice_r1.question: Frequency table: Red 12, Blue 8, Yellow 5. How many fewer students chose Yellow than Red?
hints: Think about what organize data means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how organize data works.

expected_errors.description: '더 적은' 질문에서 12+5=17 덧셈 또는 Yellow 값 5를 답으로 제출
expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.9

hints: Think about what organize data means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how organize data works.

expected_errors.description: Soccer+Basketball 대신 전체 4개 카테고리 합산 (23)
expected_errors.citation: Friel et al. (2001) p.131

hints: Think about what organize data means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how organize data works.

practice_r1.question: Tally table: Cats 𝍸𝍸|, Dogs 𝍸|||, Fish ||||. If 3 students change from Dogs to Fish, how many students would choose Fish?
hints: Think about what organize data means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how organize data works.

expected_errors.description: 가상 변경 문제에서 Dogs(8)→Dogs-3=5를 Fish에 적용하는 2단계 오류
expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.10

hints: Think about what organize data means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is A. Remember how organize data works.

expected_errors.description: Ice Cream(12)이 가장 많지만 두 번째인 Cake(10)를 선택
expected_errors.citation: Van de Walle et al. (2013) p.391

Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

hints: Think about what organize data means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is A. Remember how organize data works.

expected_errors.description: Cats와 Fish 변화 방향 역전 (Cats에 4 더하고 Fish에서 4 뺌)
expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.10 — 가상 변경 방향 오류

practice_r2.question: Frequency table: Room A – 15, Room B – 12, Room C – 18. Room A and Room B collected cans together. Room C collected cans alone. Which group collected more and by how many?
hints: Think about what organize data means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how organize data works.

expected_errors.description: Room C(18)와 A 또는 B 하나만 비교하여 잘못된 차이 계산
expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.10

practice_r2.question: Frequency table: Science Club 18, Art Club 11, Chess Club 7, Drama Club 14. How many fewer students are in Chess Club than in Drama Club?
hints: Find Drama Club and Chess Club values.
'How many fewer' means subtract.

feedback.incorrect: Find both clubs, then subtract the smaller from the larger.

expected_errors.description: '더 적게 읽은' 질문에서 빼기 대신 더하기 수행
expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.9

practice_r2.question: Frequency table: Morning 22, Afternoon 17, Evening 9, Night 2. How many more students exercise in the Morning than in the Evening?
hints: Use only Morning and Evening values.
Subtract Evening from Morning.

feedback.incorrect: You only need Morning and Evening. Subtract.

expected_errors.description: 두 카테고리 비교에 전체 합 사용
expected_errors.citation: Friel et al. (2001) p.131

expected_errors.note: 첫 번째 카테고리 값만 답으로 제출

practice_r2.question: Frequency table: Grade 3A 24, Grade 3B 18, Grade 3C 21, Grade 3D 17. How many students did NOT come from Grade 3B?
hints: First find the total.
Then subtract Grade 3B from the total.

feedback.incorrect: Step 1: Add all grades. Step 2: Subtract Grade 3B.

expected_errors.description: 전체 합계를 구한 후 2단계 계산 미완료
expected_errors.citation: EngageNY G3 M6 L2

practice_r2.question: Frequency table: Apples 8, Pears 12, Oranges 5, Mangoes 15. Which fruit was chosen the least, and what is the difference between it and the most popular fruit?
hints: Find the smallest and largest values.
Subtract smallest from largest.

feedback.incorrect: Scan all values to find the least and most, then subtract.

expected_errors.description: 최솟값 카테고리 오식별
expected_errors.citation: Van de Walle et al. (2013) p.391

practice_r2.question: Frequency table: Monday 8, Tuesday 5, Wednesday 11, Thursday 6, Friday 10. If 3 more students borrow books on Tuesday, how many books would be borrowed on Tuesday and Wednesday combined?
hints: Update Tuesday first: 5+3.
Then add the new Tuesday to Wednesday.

feedback.incorrect: First change Tuesday, THEN add to Wednesday.

expected_errors.description: 가상 증가 시나리오에서 잘못된 카테고리 업데이트
expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.10

practice_r3.question: Frequency table: Mon 7, Tue 5, Wed 9, Thu 6, Fri 3. On which two days combined did the same number of books get checked out as on Wednesday?
hints: Think about what organize data means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how organize data works.

expected_errors.description: 9와 같은 합을 찾는 대신 가장 큰 합(Mon+Tue=12) 선택
expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.10

practice_r3.question: Frequency table: Hiking 8, Camping 11, Fishing 5, Swimming 14. How many more students chose the two most popular activities combined than the two least popular combined?
practice_r3.explanation: Most popular: Swimming 14 + Camping 11 = 25. Least popular: Fishing 5 + Hiking 8 = 13. 25 − 13 = 12.
hints: Think about what organize data means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how organize data works.

expected_errors.description: 상위 2개와 하위 2개를 잘못 분류 (순위 오식별)
expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.10

practice_r3.math_note: Most popular: Swimming 14 + Camping 11 = 25. Least popular: Fishing 5 + Hiking 8 = 13. 25 − 13 = 12.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 2 Lesson 2.1 — Problem Solving: Organize Data
concept_source.description: 탤리표·빈도표로 데이터 정리 및 비교 문제 풀기

practice_r3.question: Frequency table: Fiction 16, Nonfiction 9, Poetry 5, Comics 10. The library wants each category to have at least 10 books. How many more books are needed in total?
practice_r3.explanation: Fiction: OK. Nonfiction needs 1 more. Poetry needs 5 more. Comics: OK. Total = 1 + 5 = 6.
hints: Think about what organize data means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how organize data works.

expected_errors.description: Poetry(5) 부족분만 답하고 Nonfiction(1) 누락
expected_errors.citation: EngageNY G3 M6 L2 — 복수 카테고리 합산 누락

expected_errors.note: Comics가 10이므로 추가 필요 없다고 판단하여 1만 계산

practice_r3.math_note: Fiction: OK. Nonfiction needs 1 more. Poetry needs 5 more. Comics: OK. Total = 1 + 5 = 6.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 2 Lesson 2.1 — Problem Solving: Organize Data
concept_source.description: 탤리표·빈도표로 데이터 정리 및 비교 문제 풀기

practice_r3.question: Frequency table: Juice 13, Water 8, Milk 7, Soda 2. The teacher says 'The number of students who chose Juice is the same as the number who chose Water and Milk together.' Is the teacher correct?
practice_r3.explanation: Juice = 13. Water + Milk = 8 + 7 = 15. 13 ≠ 15. The teacher is not correct.
hints: Think about what organize data means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is B. Remember how organize data works.

expected_errors.description: Water+Milk=15 계산 후 Juice(13)와의 비교 단계 누락 → '같다'고 오답
expected_errors.citation: NCTM MD Progressions K-5 (2011) p.10

practice_r3.math_note: Juice = 13. Water + Milk = 8 + 7 = 15. 13 ≠ 15. The teacher is not correct.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 2 Lesson 2.1 — Problem Solving: Organize Data
concept_source.description: 탤리표·빈도표로 데이터 정리 및 비교 문제 풀기

practice_r3.question: Tally table: Roses 𝍸𝍸|||, Tulips 𝍸||, Daisies 𝍸𝍸𝍸, Sunflowers ||||. The gardener plants 2 more of the least popular flower. What is the new total of all flowers?
practice_r3.explanation: Original total: 13+7+15+4 = 39. Least popular = Sunflowers (4). Add 2 → 39 + 2 = 41.
hints: Think about what organize data means.
Try drawing a picture or using a number line.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. The answer is C. Remember how organize data works.

expected_errors.description: Sunflowers 탤리 |||| = 4를 3 또는 5로 오독하여 최솟값 오식별
expected_errors.citation: Friel et al. (2001) p.130

practice_r3.math_note: Original total: 13+7+15+4 = 39. Least popular = Sunflowers (4). Add 2 → 39 + 2 = 41.
